title: iberis rifana
intro: Iberis rifana, also known as Rif Mountain Candytuft, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Morocco and Algeria, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties due to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Iberis rifana can be consumed as a herbal preparation, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
